yield.note: at 40° ABV after distillation and adjustment

ingredients.note: as needed to bring to final volume and strength
as needed to bring to final volume and strength

steps_en: Distill 20–27 liters of approximately 60° ABV spirit from the angelica root in 33 liters of 60° alcohol.
Bring the distillate to 50 liters at 40° ABV by adding alcohol and water as needed.
Dissolve 4 kg of sugar in water and add to the spirit.
notes_en: Double spirits (acqueviti doppie) are liqueurs with 36–50% alcohol, rich in aromatic compounds and containing little sugar (5–7%). They should be aged for a long time before consumption.

yield.note: at 40° ABV after distillation and adjustment

ingredients.note: as needed to bring to final volume and strength
as needed to bring to final volume and strength

steps_en: Distill 55 liters of 50–60° ABV spirit from the anise seeds in 67 liters of 60° alcohol.
Bring the distillate to 100 liters at 40° ABV by adding alcohol and water containing 5 kg of sugar in solution.
notes_en: Green anise is a key aromatic ingredient in double spirits.

ingredients.name_it: Scorzo d'arance o cedri o limoni
ingredients.name_en: Peel of orange, citron, or lemon
Essential oil of orange, citron, or lemon (optional)
ingredients.note: dissolved in absolute alcohol; optional enhancement

steps_en: Proceed as for Anise: distill the peels in alcohol at 60° ABV.
Bring the distillate to final volume at 40° ABV by adding alcohol and water as needed.
Sweeten with 5–7 kg of sugar according to taste.
Optional: dissolve 3 grams of essential oil of the chosen citrus fruit in absolute alcohol and add to the spirit.
notes_en: Method is identical to Anise. The essential oil enhancement is optional and should be dissolved in absolute alcohol before incorporation.
